source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly thread

quotes.quote: DO not understand this idea of swapping your ball out on the off chance that you might loose it. I have lost balls on holes with no OOB or water before, so should I just not use premium balls at all.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: The day I start to worry about loosing expensive golf balls I will start using cheap ones, until that day I will just see it as part of the expense of playing golf.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I need about 4-5 holes to settle into my swing, pretty much why it's on my mind the new ball theory. I'm not alone which is nice, maybe one day I'll relax about a new ball and combat the anxiety!!
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: It is also possible that playing lake balls and found balls is making you worse. It's a no win situation if you let either way get to you.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Best advice is to play what you can afford and try not to worry about lost ball cost.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I can play with the same ball for 5 rounds, or lose 5 in one round. I never know what is coming next. No point in getting stressed about it. It is what it is.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: The cost of a ball doesn't make you play badly - the swing you put on that ball makes you play badly.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Swapping balls depending on a tee shot? Really? Why? Seems a bit silly to me.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Well, I switched it around today and started with an old ball. I did not hit it into the water for a change, instead I lost it in the woods to the left.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I use to switch balls to an old one on our 6th which has been a real problem for me. I've stopped and simply keep the ball in play for the hole. If it's worked for five holes why would it change.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: The ball has no understanding of the swing I'm making and simply reacts so I don't get too fussed. I'm lucky enough to have a glut of balls so can get by losing a few.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I have enough to worry about, last thing on my mind is the cost of the little white thing.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I never change my ball before a hole whatever the hazard(s). I want to play freely, hence I only buy balls I can afford to lose.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I'm working on my game, I expect at some point it will be good enough to justify a premium ball, for now Srixon AD333 / Soft feel and Wilson DX2 are just fine.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Why?, Because its a mental thing. You know you've played a hole a dozen or more times and you know each time you've hit trouble and lost a ball.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: If mentally you feel you're going to hit trouble again why lose a new good ball, when you can lose an old crap one.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: The ball has no concept of the swing you'll make so it makes no difference what ball is sitting there.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I have changed balls on a tough hole or one that I struggle with and know it's a negative mindset so have stopped doing this. Stand there, put a good swing on it and trust yourself.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Friend of mine always switches to a crappy ball, sometimes an 80%? range ball, on a particular par 3 for a carry over water that's right on his limit.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: Why not? Get an even newer, better, more expensive ball out the bag and just know you are going to nail it.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: If you're worrying about the cost of them all then you can't be concentrating on the shot and will, most likely, put a bad swing on it.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: All the best will in the world isn't going to stop someone carving into a lake or whatever. If that is your natural tendency, use a poor ball for that hole until such time as you feel you are good enough to control the ball more often than not.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I normally lose a few during a round, this is being realistic given my current level - not negative.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: For me the cost of the ball makes no difference to the way I play. In comps I will use Soft Feel or AD333s, for a social or practice I will tend to use balls that I have found.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly

quotes.quote: I like positive thinking and just play the new ball regardless. Don't set up for failure.
quotes.source: Golf Monthly Forum - Is the cost of a ball making you play badly
